focusArea: Vertical padding change from py-gap-6 to py-gap-8

topPadding.notes: Desktop matches exactly. Mobile uses responsive scaling (72px) which is acceptable.

bottomPadding.notes: Desktop matches exactly. Mobile uses responsive scaling (72px) which is acceptable.

internalGaps.notes: Implementation uses gap-gap-5 (32px mobile, 48px desktop) while Figma shows 64px gap between heading/filters and content rows. This is a minor discrepancy.

issues.description: The gap between the heading section and filter/content sections is 48px in implementation vs 64px in Figma design.
issues.recommendation: Consider changing gap-gap-5 to gap-gap-6 (64px at desktop) on the inner container if exact Figma match is required. However, the current spacing looks visually balanced.

positiveFindings.description: Top and bottom padding (96px) inside dark background now matches Figma design at desktop breakpoint
Overall composition matches Figma - dark rounded background, heading with CTA button, filter bar, masonry-style card grid
Anton font headings, Roboto Medium for buttons and labels all appear correct
Dark charcoal background (#1e1e1c), white text, neon green tags are all present
Mobile layout properly stacks filters vertically and reduces card columns

compositionGate.notes: Overall shape, proportions, and element positions match the Figma design. The block displays a dark background area with proper corner radius, heading with CTA button aligned right, filter dropdowns and per-page controls, and a responsive card grid with Load More button.

notes: The primary focus of this QA - the vertical padding change from py-gap-6 to py-gap-8 - has been successfully implemented. At desktop (960px+), the padding is now 96px which matches the Figma specification of margin-7 (96px). The only discrepancy found is a minor 16px difference in internal section gaps, which does not significantly impact the overall visual design. The block passes QA.
